passage: He looked about him with growing unease. The silence was absolute, the darkness impenetrable. He had a sudden and overwhelming desire to run.

passage: He made a decision then and there. He would harbour no more resentment, make no accusations, and seize every chance that was offered to him.
source: Goodnight Mister Tom by Michelle Magorian

passage: "It's not fair," he muttered, turning away from the window. He trudged upstairs, his shoulders hunched, dragging his feet on every step. At the top, he paused and peered back down into the gloom below.
source: Tom's Midnight Garden by Philippa Pearce

questions.stem: Which precision verb means 'spoke quietly and unhappily'?
Which precision verb means 'walked slowly and heavily'?
Which precision verb means 'looked carefully into the dark'?

He ___ through the keyhole, trying to see who was inside.

passage: He was utterly exhausted and deeply troubled by what he had seen. It was bitterly cold, and yet he remained perfectly still, watching the tree line.
source: The Midnight Fox by Betsy Byars

questions.stem: She was ___ exhausted after the climb.
He was ___ troubled by what he had found.

passage: Despite every obstacle that lay before them, they pressed on with steadfast determination. It fell to Ruth, the eldest, to make provision for the younger children and to keep a watchful eye on those in her care.
source: The Silver Sword by Ian Serraillier

questions.stem: Which is the formal version of 'She kept going even though it was hard'?
options: She carried on despite the difficulty.
She pressed on despite every obstacle.
She continued even though it was tough.
She went on though it was hard.

questions.stem: Which is the formal version of 'It was his job to lead the group safely'?
options: He had to lead the group safely.
It fell to him to lead the group safely.
He was supposed to lead the group.
Leading the group was his thing.

questions.stem: Which is the formal version of 'She sorted out things for the younger children'?
options: She fixed things for the younger children.
She organised things for the younger children.
She made provision for the younger children.
She dealt with the younger children.

questions.stem: Which is the formal version of 'He watched them carefully at all times'?
options: He kept a watchful eye on them at all times.
He kept looking at them carefully.

questions.stem: Which is the formal version of 'She was really determined'?
